The entire design space is the set of engineering choices inside those three blocks, and every choice is a bet on one of two axes from the §1 tradeoff matrix: label fidelity (recovered mm-error vs. measured-exact) and embodiment gap (is the sensing rig kinematically the deployment end-effector, or a human proxy?). The blocks below give the per-system engineering breakdown the survey rows abbreviate. See also Data for the resulting datasets, Dexterous manipulation for the policies trained on them, and Tactile & hands for the force-sensing subsystems most of these rigs lack.

设计模式对比 (Design-pattern comparison)

System动作恢复方法 (action recovery)关键传感器 (key sensor)数据格式 (data format)
UMIORB-SLAM3 fork, monocular-inertial VI-SLAM (6.1mm/3.5° ATE) (primary)GoPro Hero9 + 155° Max Lens Mod fisheye + built-in IMUZarr replay buffer (fisheye kept undistorted)
FastUMIIntel RealSense T265 direct VIO, no SLAM (~10.5–24.1mm) (primary)T265 + GoPro Hero9 fisheyeROS bags @20Hz → training tensors
DexUMIPer-joint encoder→motor regression + SAM2/ProPainter inpainting (86% success) (secondary)Rotary encoders (Alps RDC506018A-class (unverified part #)) + wrist cameraInpainted video + retargeted joint cmds
Dobb·EiPhone on-board ARKit-class VIO + LiDAR (no ATE published) (primary)iPhone 12 Pro+ RGB + LiDAR + IMU (Record3D).r3d → 256×256 imgs + absolute 6D pose
DexCapPer-glove SLAM cam 6-DoF + EMF finger mocap (occlusion-robust) (secondary)Rokoko EMF gloves + RealSense L515 + 3× T26560Hz mocap+RGBD, DexIL format
ALOHA 2Measured — leader-arm joint encoders (exact) (primary)4× RealSense D405 + ViperX/WidowX encodersHDF5 14-DoF @50Hz (LeRobot-portable)
GELLOMeasured — kinematically-matched leader encoders (12-bit, 0.088°) (secondary)DYNAMIXEL XL330 servos as encoders + robot camsHDF5 joint-space, per-robot
DROIDMeasured — Franka proprioception + Quest 2 6-DoF target (primary)2× ZED 2 + ZED Mini stereo + Franka encodersRLDS + raw trajectory.h5 (.svo video)
Open-TeleVision / VRMeasured — VR SE(3) hand/head/wrist → dex-retarget (SLSQP) (secondary)Apple Vision Pro / Quest + active stereo head camRobot-native HDF5 + retargeted joints
Aria / egocentric + glovesHand-pose only (no robot action) / on-device VIO+eye-track (primary)Aria Gen2: 12MP RGB + 4 CV cams + 2 IMU + eye/PPGVRS (Aria) / vendor mocap (gloves)

UMI (Universal Manipulation Interface, 通用操作接口)

  • 系统组成 (composition): 3D-printed (PLA) parallel-jaw gripper body (2-finger, trigger-free, spring/pulley close) + GoPro Hero9 Black as the sole sensor/recorder + GoPro Max Lens Mod (155° fisheye) screwed on + two rear-facing acrylic/mirror strips in the camera's peripheral FoV for "implicit stereo" side-view + a fiducial (checkerboard/dot) marker printed inside the jaws to make finger-width visible to the same feed + soft/compliant 3D-printed fingers (TPU-tipped, series-elastic principle) + GoPro's built-in 6-axis IMU logged to the .mp4 GPMF track (no separate IMU chip) + GoPro Labs firmware for QR-triggered hardware start/stop sync + GoPro's internal battery. (primary) Actuated fork replaces manual spring-close with a DYNAMIXEL XL430-W250-T servo + U2D2 interface/power hub + MGN7C 80mm linear rails + 5mm flat belt + constant-force springs (0.1×8×110). (secondary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): GoPro Hero9 + Max Lens Mod, 155° diagonal FoV, up to 1080p/2.7K in MAX SuperView — this single fisheye feed does double duty: policy RGB observation and VI-SLAM pose recovery. IMU: GoPro internal 6-axis (~200Hz gyro/accel) muxed into the GPMF metadata track of the same MP4 — the "hardware sync" trick: video + IMU + audio share one hardware clock → zero cross-sensor latency to calibrate. Mirrors: 2× planar mirrors bracket-mounted ~45° reflect the gripper side into the same frame → stereo-like depth from one monocular camera. Fiducial: printed dot pattern inside the jaw, regressed offline to continuous jaw width (no encoder in base design). (primary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): 2-DoF functional unit — jaw open/close is the only actuated DoF; the "6-DoF" comes from free-hand wrist motion recovered by SLAM, not mechanical sensing. Weight ~780g; dims ~310×175×210mm; finger stroke 80mm chosen to match WSG-50/Robotiq grippers so trajectories replay directly on a UR5e/Franka fitted with identical gripper geometry (this is the whole embodiment-gap-closing trick). GoPro on a rigid printed cage → camera-to-tip transform fixed, calibrated once. (primary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Capture — human hand-holds the gripper, GoPro records fisheye RGB + IMU + jaw-fiducial as one MP4/demo; GoPro Labs QR-flash hardware-syncs multiple units. Processing — batch offline SLAM in Docker (chicheng/orb_slam3, Steffen Urban's GoPro-tuned ORB-SLAM3 fork) ingests fisheye+IMU in monocular-inertial mode → per-frame 6-DoF gripper pose; intrinsics/IMU-cam extrinsics pre-calibrated once via OpenImuCameraCalibrator. Action-label recovery — SLAM poses = ground-truth EE trajectory; jaw width regressed separately from the fiducial crop (small ConvNet, Dobb·E-style). Reported tracking error: ~6.1mm / 3.5° per-gripper absolute ATE, ~10.1mm / 0.8° inter-gripper RPE (relative pose error between the two grippers, not inter-frame) (primary, paper-reported, Fig. 12). Latency-matching: a filmed QR/flash code measures GoPro-to-GoPro and camera-to-robot latency empirically → actions shifted/interpolated at inference so runtime obs-action lag matches training. Format: SE(3) relative (not absolute) pose actions + continuous gripper-width channel, packed into a Zarr replay buffer; frames kept as the original undistorted fisheye (policy sees fisheye directly, no rectification). Policy: Diffusion Policy w/ CLIP-pretrained ViT-L/14. (primary)
  • 软件栈 (software): ORB-SLAM3 (Steffen-Urban fork, Dockerized); OpenImuCameraCalibrator; scripts_slam_pipeline/ in real-stanford/universal_manipulation_interface (~99.9% Python, Conda) → Zarr → Diffusion Policy (PyTorch). Robot side: 3Dconnexion SpaceMouse + UR5/UR5e or Franka driver. Actuated variant: Dynamixel SDK over U2D2. (primary)
  • 成本 (BOM): gripper (printed body + soft fingers + screws + mirrors + fiducial) ~$73; GoPro Hero9 + Max Lens Mod + mounts ~$298; total ~$371 (paper figures) (primary). Actuated fork adds XL430 servo (~$50–70) + U2D2 (~$40–50) + 2× MGN7C rails + belt + springs — community "low hundreds added", no published total (unverified).
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): GoPro chosen over a custom cam+IMU board because it's off-the-shelf, ruggedized, and — critically — bundles a hardware-synced IMU with video, killing a whole class of timestamp-fusion bugs cheaply. 155° fisheye trades center resolution for peripheral context: keeps fingertips + object in frame under large wrist rotation and feeds ORB-SLAM3's wide-baseline tracking. Mirrors = cheap implicit stereo with no second camera/cable/sync. Soft fingers absorb hand jitter and generalize contact. Relative SE(3) actions so the same policy works on fixed or mobile base and small SLAM drift doesn't compound. The authors' own stated most-fragile part: ORB-SLAM3 tracking loss/drift in textureless / fast-motion scenes. (primary)

FastUMI (Fast Universal Manipulation Interface)

  • 系统组成 (composition): Two-part design — a handheld collect unit and a robot-mounted infer unit — deliberately hardware-decoupled across ~5 grippers (xArm, Robotiq 2F-85 …) vs. UMI's WSG-50 lock-in. Handheld = Intel RealSense T265 tracking module + GoPro Hero9 fisheye + fingertip fiducials for aperture + Elgato HD60 X capture card. Dual-arm variant = 2× T265 + 2× GoPro. (primary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): RealSense T265 — a self-contained VIO module (dual fisheye + built-in IMU + on-chip pose) outputting direct 6-DoF pose, no external SLAM. GoPro Hero9 fisheye at 1280×720 @60fps for policy RGB. Fingertip fiducials → gripper aperture. (primary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): UMI-class parallel-jaw handheld, but drops UMI's side mirrors to free gripper space (loses implicit-stereo depth). Because it's gripper-agnostic, the mechanical body is re-printed per target gripper rather than locked to one. (primary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Capture via ROS @20Hz. Action-label recovery: T265 emits pose directly → zero SLAM calibration, but task-dependent ~10.5mm (best, "pick cup") to ~24.1mm (worst, "rearrange coke") position error — coarser than UMI's 6.1mm (the oft-cited "~2.37cm" sits near the high end). (primary) FastUMI-100K corpus (arXiv 2510.08022): >100k trajectories, 54 tasks, 120–500 frames/traj; commonly-quoted "~600h / ~3 months" is (unverified — not in the abstract).
  • 软件栈 (software): ROS-based capture; Elgato capture card for the GoPro HDMI stream; off-the-shelf T265 SDK for pose. (primary)
  • 成本 (BOM): not disclosed; off-the-shelf T265 + GoPro ≈ "several hundred $/unit", claimed cheaper in aggregate than SLAM/MoCap builds (company claim).
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): A direct throughput-vs-fidelity trade — cm-level zero-calibration tracking swaps out mm-level SLAM. Matters for precision insertion, but eliminates UMI's per-device IMU-cam calibration burden entirely. Note: Intel discontinued the T265, a real supply risk for any T265-based rig going forward. (primary)

DexUMI (dexterous exoskeleton hand interface)

  • 系统组成 (composition): A wearable exoskeleton glove — a rigid 3D-printed link-and-joint mechanism strapped over the human fingers — instrumented with one rotary encoder per actuated joint measuring finger flexion/extension directly on the exo linkage; plus a wrist-mounted camera placed where the target robot hand's wrist camera will sit. Validated on two robot hands: underactuated 6-DoF Inspire Hand and fully-actuated 12-DoF XHand. (secondary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): Rotary encoders (Alps RDC506018A-class cited in the sensing lineage) — one per actuated joint, reading human joint angles mechanically, not via vision → immune to the self-occlusion that breaks vision-only hand-pose estimators (HaMeR-style). Single wrist camera (model/res not disclosed) → training RGB after inpainting. No tactile sensor in base DexUMI (tactile variants are follow-on work). (secondary; unverified encoder count)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): Rigid printed exo, DoF matched to the target robot hand (up to 12 for XHand, 6 for Inspire) so each encoder maps ~1:1 to a robot finger-joint command. Worn like a brace (not gripped like UMI); the operator's own wrist supplies the 6-DoF wrist motion (tracked like a UMI wrist cam). (secondary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Capture — operator wears the exo and works at natural speed with direct haptic/proprioceptive feedback; encoders stream joint angles synchronized to wrist video. Action-label recovery — a regression model (calibrated per-joint from paired canonical poses) maps encoder values → robot motor commands, compensating for backlash/friction nonlinearity; paper notes precision is typically guaranteed in only one direction (open OR close) per joint — a disclosed hardware limitation. Visual-gap closure — recorded video is segmented with SAM2, the hand+exo pixels erased, and the region filled by ProPainter flow-based video inpainting (optionally compositing the robot hand) so training frames resemble the deployed robot's own camera view. Result: 86% avg task success across the two hands; claimed 3.2× data-collection efficiency vs. teleop. (secondary)
  • 软件栈 (software): SAM2 (Ravi et al. 2024) segmentation + ProPainter (Zhou et al. 2023) inpainting + a per-joint regression model (architecture not disclosed) → downstream imitation-learning policy. real-stanford/DexUMI repo. (secondary)
  • 成本 (BOM): not disclosed in accessible sources (no published BOM table); likely low-hundreds-to-low-thousands DIY by UMI norms (unverified).
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): Solves the kinematic gap between human and robot hand by sensing joints mechanically (occlusion-immune) and enabling natural-speed haptic demos → the 3.2× efficiency claim. The trade this creates is a visual gap (training frames show a chunky exo, not the robot hand), answered entirely in software (SAM2+ProPainter) to keep the wearable cheap. The need for both a mechanical remap and a visual inpaint is an implicit admission the naive approach fails; inpainting injects its own fidelity risk (synthetic hand ≠ ground truth). (secondary)

Dobb·E ("The Stick" + iPhone)

  • 系统组成 (composition): "The Stick" = a $25 off-the-shelf Vive suction-cup reacher-grabber as the mechanical backbone; stock padded tips swapped for 3D-printed cylindrical tips (better on thin handles); a 3D-printed iPhone mount (top/bottom/knob/hex-nut border, M5 socket screws) clamped near the pivot; an iPhone 12 Pro or newer (LiDAR required) running the Record3D app as the entire sensing/recording package — no separate camera, IMU chip, or SLAM computer on the tool. For deployment, the same mount bolts an iPhone to a Hello Robot Stretch RE1 wrist; an onboard Intel NUC runs the policy, streaming RGB-D over USB. (primary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): iPhone 12 Pro+ built-in stack — rear RGB @1280×720, rear LiDAR depth @256×192, internal IMU/gyro fused by Apple's on-board ARKit-class VIO → 6D pose (trans+rot) @30fps, all via Record3D's proprietary .r3d format. No external SLAM, no markers. Gripper open/close is NOT physically sensed — recovered purely visually in post. (primary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): spring-loaded trigger squeeze → 1-DoF pincer; rigid iPhone mount fixes phone-to-jaw geometry so the phone's 6D pose stands in for gripper pose. Same mount geometry reused on the Stretch RE1 wrist → sensor viewpoint consistent between human demo and robot execution. (primary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Capture — operator walks a home holding the Stick recording first-person RGB-D + 6D pose @30fps into .r3d; ~10 min/home over a 5×5/4×6 start grid → "Homes of New York" (HoNY): 22 homes, 5,620 demos, 13h, ~1.5M frames. Action-label recovery — 6D pose from the iPhone's hardware VIO, explicitly chosen over classical SfM (OpenSfM) because "SfM fails as soon as the camera gets very close to any surface and loses all visual features" (a tested-and-rejected alternative). Gripper-state recovery — a 3-layer ConvNet trained on 500 hand-labeled frames regresses continuous [0,1] tip-distance from RGB → 0.035 MSE on held-out val (the learned analog to UMI's fiducial). Format — imgs 256×256; actions stored as absolute 6D iPhone pose @30Hz, converted to relative deltas at train/runtime; final 7D action (3 trans + 3 axis-angle + 1 gripper). Representation — ResNet34 self-supervised with MoCo-v3, 60 epochs on HoNY → "Home Pretrained Representations" (HPR), beating R3M/VC-1/MVP/ImageNet by ≥23%. Policy — 2 FC layers on the 512-d HPR embedding → 7D action, per-axis-normalized MSE, lr 3e-5, 50 epochs, subsampled 3.75Hz. Deploy — NUC @30Hz → PyKDL IK solver that adds the camera frame as a joint directly in the URDF (no explicit camera-to-EE calibration at runtime). 81% success across 109 tasks in 10 novel homes. (primary)
  • 软件栈 (software): Record3D (commercial iOS app) for capture; custom Python .r3d→tensor pipeline; MoCo-v3 pretraining (PyTorch); PyKDL IK. (primary)
  • 成本 (BOM): ~$25 stick + user's own iPhone — the cheapest rig in this survey. (primary)
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): ARKit-class on-device VIO removes UMI's entire SLAM-tuning burden but ties fidelity to Apple's black-box tracker (no published ATE). A reacher-grabber's kinematics differ from a robot arm even more than UMI's WSG-50-matched jaw → larger embodiment gap. Wins on dead-simple in-home scale; loses on precision/force/exact-kinematics tasks. (primary)

DexCap (mocap-glove chest rig)

  • 系统组成 (composition): Portable body-worn rig — Rokoko EMF gloves (finger joints) + one SLAM camera per glove (6-DoF wrist pose) + a RealSense L515 LiDAR on a 3D-printed chest harness + 3× RealSense T265 below + Intel NUC + power bank in a backpack; runs @60Hz. (secondary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): Rokoko EMF (electromagnetic-field) gloves for finger joint angles — occlusion-robust at the moment of grasp, a real edge over vision-only hand tracking. Per-glove SLAM camera for wrist 6-DoF; L515 solid-state LiDAR for chest-frame 3D scene context; 3× T265 for VIO. (secondary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): no gripper — the human hand is the end-effector; the chest harness (3D-printed) fixes L515/T265 geometry. The EMF glove supplies dexterous finger DoF that a UMI parallel jaw cannot. (secondary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Capture — "in the wild" daily-activity capture, ~40 min/session (backpack battery cap). Action-label recovery — per-glove SLAM cam gives wrist SE(3); EMF gloves give finger joints directly; L515+T265 place the hands in a scene frame. Training — the DexIL imitation algorithm learns dexterous skills from the fused mocap+RGBD. (secondary)
  • 软件栈 (software): Rokoko SDK (EMF); RealSense SDK (L515/T265); DexIL training stack. (secondary)
  • 成本 (BOM): ~$3.1k–$3.5k hardware (Rokoko-based build; exact figure not in paper) (unverified).
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): EMF finger tracking is occlusion-robust where vision fails (grasp contact) — but glove mocap ≠ robot-hand joint limits (the same dexterous embodiment gap as DexUMI, without the exo's mechanical remap). Chest LiDAR gives 3D scene context most handhelds lack; the 40-min battery caps session length. (secondary)

ALOHA 2 (teleoperation leader-follower workcell)

  • 系统组成 (composition): Bimanual parallel-jaw workcell — 2× ViperX 6-DoF followers + 2× WidowX leaders (Trossen Robotics), kinematically matched; a low-friction rail for ergonomic leading; 4× RealSense D405 cameras (2× wrist + overhead + worm's-eye); a MuJoCo sim twin. (primary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): 4× RealSense D405 short-range RGB-D. Action state comes from the leader-arm joint encoders (Dynamixel-based ViperX/WidowX) — measured, not recovered. (primary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): leader (WidowX) and follower (ViperX) share kinematics → direct joint-space mapping, no IK; the human back-drives the low-friction leader and the follower mirrors it. 14-DoF total (2×7). (primary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Capture — human back-drives leaders; follower joint positions logged @50Hz + gripper aperture = the exact action label (zero-to-small embodiment gap, gold-standard fidelity). Format — 14-DoF joint positions @50Hz stored as HDF5 episodes (the ALOHA/ACT convention), directly portable to LeRobot. (primary)
  • 软件栈 (software): ALOHA/ACT open-source stack; MuJoCo twin for sim eval; HDF5 → LeRobot for community training. (primary)
  • 成本 (BOM): ~$17.4k arms (2× ViperX ≈ $11k + 2× WidowX ≈ $6.4k) → ~$22k complete stationary 4-arm build (cameras/frame/PC). The ~$32k figure adds a Mobile-ALOHA base (~$10k AgileX Tracer) and is not a stationary price. The paper itself gives no dollar figure (cost is third-party BOM). (primary hardware; secondary cost)
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): Zero-to-small embodiment gap + exact measured joint actions = the gold standard for label fidelity. The wall is cost (~$22k/station) + one human per station + the ~35 demos/h fatigue ceiling — operator-bound, not hardware-bound. The de-facto academic standard and the template for Chinese data-center rigs. See Manipulation. (primary)

GELLO (kinematically-matched leader arm)

  • 系统组成 (composition): A low-cost leader arm built as a kinematic replica of the target follower, so each leader joint maps 1:1 to a follower joint (no IK, intuitive joint-space teleop). Structure = 3D-printed links custom-matched to the target robot + a chain of DYNAMIXEL servos used purely as high-resolution absolute encoders (passively back-driven by the operator). (secondary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): DYNAMIXEL XL330-series servos as the joint sensors — 12-bit encoders, ~0.088° mechanical resolution — read as the action label; robot's own cameras supply observations. (secondary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): printed links reproduce the follower's link lengths/joint axes so the leader is a scale kinematic twin; the operator hand-guides the leader, follower mirrors joint angles. (secondary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Action-label recovery = measured — leader encoder angles ARE the follower command; no SLAM/VIO, no post-hoc estimation. Format — joint-space trajectories, HDF5 per robot; force-augmented forks (FACTR, "GELLO+Force") add gripper F/T for contact-rich learning. (secondary)
  • 软件栈 (software): Dynamixel SDK; open GELLO codebase; per-robot URDF for the printed twin. (secondary)
  • 成本 (BOM): ~$270 (original single-arm BOM) to ~$363 per leader arm in later builds — dominated by the Dynamixel servos. (secondary)
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): The cheapest path to exact, measured, joint-space labels — trading UMI's robot-free economics for a hard kinematic match (needs a printed twin per target robot, and a follower robot present at collection). Wins on label fidelity + zero-IK intuitiveness; loses on portability (one twin per arm) and on force (base design is vision+proprioception only, hence the FACTR force forks). (secondary)

DROID (Franka + Quest 2 VR rig)

  • 系统组成 (composition): A standardized single-rig design replicated across 13 institutions — Franka Panda 7-DoF arm + Meta Quest 2 controllers for 6-DoF EE + gripper teleop + 2× ZED 2 stereo + 1× ZED Mini (wrist) cameras on a portable height-adjustable desk. (primary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): ZED 2 / ZED Mini stereo (full-HD, 3 views + depth); Franka joint encoders for proprioception; Quest 2 controllers for the 6-DoF teleop target. (primary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): Franka 7-DoF follower; VR gives EE-space (not joint) teleop — the operator moves a Quest controller, IK drives the arm. Standardized desk → high scene diversity per calibrated setup. (primary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Capture @15Hz, camera data stored in ZED .svo (converted to .mp4 for release). Action-label recovery = measured — each trajectory logs 7D joint pos+vel, 6D EE pose+vel (base frame), 1D gripper pos+vel; the DROID base policy outputs joint-velocity commands (action space kept in joint space to avoid compounding errors from Cartesian conversion). Format — released in RLDS (TFDS-style, full-HD stereo + all low-dim) and raw trajectory.h5 (HDF5 low-dim + .svo video). 76k trajectories / 350h / 564 scenes / 86 tasks over 12 months. (primary)
  • 软件栈 (software): ZED SDK (.svo); DROID data pipeline (droid_dataset_builder) → RLDS/TFDS; Franka + Quest 2 drivers. (primary)
  • 成本 (BOM): Franka ~$25–30k + Quest 2 ~$300 + ZED cams — robot-bound (the arm dominates cost). (primary)
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): One standardized calibrated rig → maximal scene diversity and cross-lab reproducibility; VR gives cheap 6-DoF teleop but only EE-space control (no direct joint puppeting). Clean measured labels; robot-bound and operator-bound. RLDS makes it the reference in-the-wild manipulation dataset. See Data. (primary)

Open-TeleVision / VR immersive teleop (incl. iDP3, AgiBot VR)

  • 系统组成 (composition): A VR headset (Apple Vision Pro or Quest) streams the operator's hand + head + wrist poses in SE(3) to a server that does human→robot retargeting; a head-mounted stereo camera on the robot actively follows the operator's head for immersive first-person feedback. Applied to humanoids (e.g. Fourier GR1 with Inspire Hands in iDP3; Unitree/G1). (secondary)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): VR device on-board hand/head/wrist tracking (SE(3)); robot active stereo head camera for egocentric observation; iDP3 adds a head RealSense L515 LiDAR for 3D point-cloud policy input (which added ~0.5s teleop latency). (secondary; iDP3 primary)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): teleoperates a real deployment robot (zero embodiment gap). Dexterous hands sync via 7 keypoint vectors; grippers via a single thumb-index vector. (secondary)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Action-label recovery = measured on-robot — VR SE(3) targets → dex-retargeting optimization (SLSQP) → robot joint commands, logged with the robot's own proprioception. Format — robot-native HDF5 + retargeted joint trajectories, typically LeRobot-portable. Reported: up to 100% success on towel-fold / can-sort (Open-TeleVision); iDP3 collected only 10 demos/task. (secondary)
  • 软件栈 (software): dex-retargeting library (SLSQP real-time optimization); OpenTeleVision codebase; robot-specific controllers. (secondary)
  • 成本 (BOM): Apple Vision Pro ~$3,500 (or Quest ~$300) + the deployment humanoid (tens of $k). (secondary; AVP price primary)
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): Zero embodiment gap (data is on the deployment robot) and immersive active vision → high-precision dexterous teleop — but near-zero scalability: iDP3's authors state verbatim that AVP teleop is "tiring for human teleoperators, making imitation data hard to scale up within the research lab." This is the exact opposite pole from UMI and the clearest illustration of why the field pays for robot-free capture despite the gap. (secondary; iDP3 fatigue quote primary)

Aria / egocentric glasses + data gloves / mocap suits (passive human capture)

  • 系统组成 (composition): Passive human-worn sensors that record demonstrations with no robot and no explicit action label — head-worn glasses (Meta Project Aria, Apple Vision Pro egocentric/EgoDex, Tesla's 5-camera helmet+backpack), instrumented data gloves (Generalist "data hands", Genesis tactile e-skin glove), and full-body mocap suits (Neura's 1,000+ industrial workers). (primary for Aria/EgoDex; company-reported for gloves/Tesla)
  • 传感器栈 (sensors): Aria Gen 2 — 12MP RGB + 4 CV/SLAM cameras (front stereo, HDR) + 2 IMUs + magnetometer + barometer + GNSS + eye-tracking (per-eye gaze, vergence, pupil, blink) + PPG heart-rate + 7-mic array + contact mic; on-device SLAM/eye/hand tracking on custom silicon. EgoDex (Vision Pro): on-device hand/finger tracking → 829h + paired 3D hand joints, 194 tasks. Genesis glove: tactile-sensing e-skin claiming a 1:1:1 glove↔human-hand↔robot-hand mapping. (Aria/EgoDex primary; Genesis company-reported)
  • 机械/硬件 (mechanical): no gripper/arm — the human body is the effector; glasses are worn, gloves fit the hand, suits fit the body. Genesis claims "bone-for-bone" glove↔robot-hand geometry. (mixed)
  • 数据管线 (pipeline): Action-label recovery — Aria/EgoDex yield hand-pose only, no robot action labels, no force; retargeting hand→gripper/robot-hand is the open bridge (EgoMimic co-training: 90 min Aria → up to 400% gain). Gloves add finger joints (+ tactile for Genesis). Mocap suits add full-body joints. Format — Aria: VRS multi-stream container; gloves/suits: vendor mocap formats; all need a downstream retarget step to become robot actions. (Aria/EgoMimic primary; glove claims company-reported)
  • 软件栈 (software): projectaria_tools (VRS, on-device SLAM/eye/hand); Rokoko/vendor mocap SDKs; Genesis/Generalist proprietary stacks. (mixed)
  • 成本 (BOM): Aria = research device (not retail-priced); Apple Vision Pro ~$3,500; Generalist/Genesis gloves "cheap"/"100× less" (company claim, unverified); Tesla/Neura not disclosed. (mixed)
  • 关键设计取舍 (design read): Cheapest, most-scalable capture (passive, crowd-sourceable, environment-diverse) — the entire 2025–26 wearables pivot (Generalist 500k+ h, Figure Go-Big, NVIDIA EgoScale) bets cheap human data at volume beats expensive robot data. The structural weakness: hand-pose-only, no measured robot action, and (except Genesis/DexUMI/DexCap) no force/tactile — the one signal video cannot fake. See Data foundry and Tactile & hands. (mixed; wearables corpus figures company-reported)

来源: https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10329 · https://umi-gripper.github.io/ · https://github.com/real-stanford/universal_manipulation_interface · https://github.com/actuated-umi/actuated-umi-gripper · https://roboticsproceedings.org/rss20/p045.pdf · https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.19499 · https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.08022 · https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.21864 · https://dex-umi.github.io/ · https://github.com/real-stanford/DexUMI · https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.16098 · https://dobb-e.com/ · https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.07788 · https://dex-cap.github.io/ · https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.02292 · https://www.roboticscenter.ai/blog/aloha-robot-guide · https://arxiv.org/html/2309.13037v2 · https://arxiv.org/html/2507.13602v1 · https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.12945 · https://droid-dataset.github.io/droid/the-droid-dataset · https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.01512 · https://github.com/OpenTeleVision/TeleVision · https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10803 · https://machinelearning.apple.com/research/egodex-learning-dexterous-manipulation · https://ai.meta.com/blog/egomimic-project-aria-georgia-tech-ego4d-robotics-embodied-ai/ · https://www.meta.com/blog/project-aria-gen-2-next-generation-egocentric-research-glasses-reality-labs-ai-robotics/ · https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/genesis-ai-unveils-gene-26-5--the-first-ai-brain-to-enable-robots-with-human-level-physical-manipulation-capabilities-302763638.html

3. Player-by-player — which devices each major player uses

PlayerCollection device(s)Scale / facilityNotes
Physical Intelligence (company-pi)In-house teleop (family B/C); staged environments, rented Airbnbs; also a Scale AI customerπ0-era ~10k h (unverified); π0.5 + ~400 h mobile-manipTeleop for clean last-mile labels; π*0.6/RECAP fine-tunes on teleop corrections. Does not sell data.
Figure (company-figure)In-house multi-operator teleop fleet + Go-Big egocentric human video (family A+B)Helix ~500 h teleop; Go-Big taps 100k+ residential unitsVLM auto-labels teleop clips (hindsight language) — annotation pushed to GPUs. Claims 100%-human-video zero-shot transfer.
1X (company-1x)On-robot teleop "Expert Mode" in real homes (family C) + VR teleopHome-deployed NEO fleetTeleop sessions double as training data ("It's also useful data for us" — Børnich); Waymo-style flywheel.
Tesla Optimus (company-optimus)5-camera helmet/backpack egocentric rig (family A, since 2025-06) + Optimus fleet; dropped mocap suitsSalaried operators, Palo AltoVision-only pivot mirrors FSD camera-first playbook; no force/tactile in the human data.
Generalist AI (data-foundry)Wrist-worn "data hands" wearable (family A) via contracted foundry partners500k+ h, +10k h/week; thousands of homes/warehousesVertically integrated; the purest UMI-economics-at-scale bet.
Skild AISim-heavy + human video + teleop mix (details thin) (unverified)—Public device detail limited in surfaced sources.
Genesis AITactile data glove, 1:1:1 human↔glove↔robot-hand (family A)GENE-26.5, 2026-05Claims 100× cheaper, 5× more usable data; all company-reported.
Scale AI (data-foundry)Broker: teleop + human-video + eval; UR AI Trainer leader-follower kit into UR's ~100k arm base100k+ production hours, SF lab + globalSells collection-as-a-service; buyers own their data.
AgiBot (company-agibot)A2 VR + whole-body mocap on 100× G1 (family C/D)~4,000 m² Lingang; "tens of thousands of records/day" (press) (daily rate unverified)Source of open AgiBot World (1M+ trajectories, ~2,976 h, 217 tasks); F/T-instrumented.
UnitreeVR full-body teleop on G1 humanoid (family D); NVIDIA GR00T stackHumanoid shipments 5,500+ (2025)Sells the robot + teleop; less a data-factory, more a platform vendor. See Manipulation.
Apptronik (data-foundry)On-robot Apollo fleets under mixed teleop/autonomy (family C)Robot Park, 90,000 sq ft AustinWork-as-data-collection; feeds DeepMind Gemini Robotics; Mercedes/GXO satellite sites.
Academic (Stanford/Columbia/NYU/CMU)UMI, DexUMI, DexCap, Dobb·E, ActiveUMI (family A); ALOHA/DROID (family B/D)Lab-to-in-the-wildThe R&D engine for robot-free capture; nearly every family-A device originates here.

中国数据采集生态 (China Data-Collection Ecosystem)

China is a structurally distinct data-collection story, not just a cheaper copy of the Western one. Three forces stack: (1) state-backed 数据训练场 (national/municipal "training grounds" that socialize collection cost across government, hardware vendors, and cheap labor — 40+ announced, ~two dozen operational as of 2026-07); (2) cheap teleop labor (JD gig home-collection documented at 20 yuan / $3 per hour; only the $3 gig figure is source-confirmed — broader "$3–20/h" ranges are cited loosely; vs $20–50/h US — see Data foundry); and (3) unmatched hardware-vendor density — the same supply chain that makes 63–70% of the world's humanoid components (see Landscape: China) also cost-reduces the collection rigs themselves (AgileX turned Mobile-ALOHA into a €16k catalog SKU; a dozen vendors now sell purpose-built data-collection gloves/grippers/egocentric kits). The result is a market where the ALOHA/UMI/DexCap designs from the device matrix reappear as commodity Chinese products, and where open dataset releases (AgiBot World, Galaxea Open-World, RoboMIND) are used as ecosystem subsidies the way Chinese LLM labs use open weights. This section covers the Chinese players and their rigs; for device physics see data-collection-devices, for facility economics see data-foundry, for company/funding context see landscape-china and company-agibot.

Scoreboard (as of 2026-07)

Legend for 类型: 整机厂 = full-robot maker; 硬件供应商 = collection-rig/sensor vendor; 数据基建 = data infrastructure (facility / synthetic / service).

公司 (Company)类型数据采集方案 / 设备 (device)规模 (scale)一句话批判 (one-line critical read)
星海图 Galaxea AI整机厂 + 数据R1 Lite 23-DoF mobile-bimanual rig, isomorphic teleop (not VR-retarget); own A1 arms100k trajectories / 500+ h / 50 scenes / 58 skills; open (HF + arXiv 2509.00576)Most independently verifiable Chinese release (real paper + dataset card); collects on the same R1 Lite you can buy → small embodiment gap; but 500 h is modest vs AgiBot's ~3,000 h, and the "400k downloads" figure is press-only — the HF card itself shows only ~13k downloads/month (2026-07), so 400k is unverified and likely inflated.
智元 AgiBot整机厂 + 数据基建100+ G1 humanoids; VR controller + whole-body mocap teleop; Lingang/Zhangjiang halls; Genie Studio platformAgiBot World: 1M+ trajectories / ~2,976 h / 217 tasks; "30k–50k episodes/day" (press)The scale leader and the open-data anchor (IROS'25 best-paper finalist); but the daily-throughput and camera-brand (Orbbec vs RealSense/Livox) details are single-source/inconsistent — treat facility numbers as company-framed. (unverified: daily rate) See company-agibot.
银河通用 Galbot整机厂 + 合成数据No teleop factory — sim/render pipeline (domain randomization); real capture rides deployed G1 in stores/pharmacySynGrasp-1B (~1B synthetic grasp frames); ~10 stores + 1 pharmacy (300+ orders/day)The clearest synthetic-first bet — useful counterpoint, but the thinnest on real-data disclosure (no trajectory/hour count); founder He Wang himself concedes real/RL data still needed for reliability → "synthetic-only" is marketing-forward. (company-reported)
千寻智能 Spirit AI整机厂 + 数据基建Proprietary wearable collection devices, 7th gen (lightweight/low-cost); distributed 100+-city fleet (not one factory)"~1,000 devices across 100+ cities", closed-loop pipeline; no public trajectory/hour countDistributed-collection architecture is a genuine contrast to AgiBot's centralized hall — but zero verifiable dataset artifact; RMB ~4.5bn in ~3 months reads as 2026 funding froth. (company-reported / unverified)
自变量 X Square Robot整机厂 + 数据Self-collected real-robot data + open sets; end-to-end WALL modelsWALL-OSS open weights (4.2B, runs on RTX 4090); trajectory count undisclosedModel-first; data scale not disclosed as trajectory/hour figures — verify against the open-weight release, not the RMB-20B valuation headline. (company-reported) See landscape-china.
逐际动力 LimX Dynamics整机厂"Data Recipe" multi-source (teleop + sim + human video); DreamActor pipeline; consumer-camera scene scans (no bespoke rig)Not disclosed — no dataset/paper/facility figureThinnest disclosure in the set; "Data Recipe"/"DreamActor" are pipeline-level positioning language only — treat as marketing until a technical report surfaces. (unverified)
穹彻智能 Noematrix整机厂 (cross-embodiment)"CoMiner" companion-style continuous collection + "embodiment-free (无本体)" collection — no public hardware specNot disclosedNames but never spec's its devices; do not conflate with 千寻智能/Spirit AI (separate firm) — the two were listed ambiguously as "千寻/穹彻". (unverified / single-source)
ByteDance Seed (GR-3 / ByteMini)整机厂 (self-embodiment) + 大厂PICO 4 Ultra VR for cheap human-hand data + Meta Quest whole-body robot teleop; automated collection scheduler; ByteMini 22-DoFPer-task: 35k robot traj / 69 h pick-place; 101 h table-bussing; 116 h cloth; VR human ~450/h vs robot ~250/hBest-documented methodology (arXiv 2507.15493, exact device names + collection-rate math); honest about the human-VR embodiment gap (no joint/gripper state) — but closed (no public dataset). (primary)
AgileX 松灵 (Cobot Magic)硬件供应商De-facto Chinese Mobile-ALOHA: Tracer base + 4× PiPER 6-DoF arms (leader-follower) + Orbbec depth + 2 wrist/1 top camSold as catalog product; €15.6k–€46.2k by config; used across Chinese collection centersThe commodity that made teleop-rig access trivial — but same clean-label/operator-bound ceiling (~35 demos/h class) as ALOHA 2; sells the picks, not the data. (vendor-verified; PiPER arm reach 626 mm per AgileX, not 610)
帕西尼 PaXini硬件供应商 (触觉)Multi-dim tactile dexterous hands / gloves (DexH13, DexH5, GMH18) usable as teleop + tactile-capture front-endsDexH13: 1,140 tactile units / 3,420 channels, 16 DoF, 8MP hand-eye cam; real-time teleopAdds the one signal video-based family-A rigs structurally can't get (force/tactile — see device matrix); but tactile-in-training data is still niche and its "world-first" framing is vendor PR. (company-reported)
GenRobot 简智新创硬件供应商Pure-play collection-device vendor: DAS Ego (egocentric), DAS Controller (isomorphic teleop), DAS Gripper (handheld/UMI-class), DAS Dex, DAS Fingers (tactile)10,000+ h data across 50+ scenarios; Gen Matrix governance platformThis is what "genrobot" resolves to (see below) — a Chinese arms-dealer mirroring the whole family A/B/C device taxonomy as buyable SKUs; specs/prices not public → verify before quoting. (company-reported)
它石智航 Morphic (SenseHub)硬件供应商 + 数据SenseHub / TARS ultra-light wearable: TARS-Vision (eyes) + TARS-Glove (hands); "what you see is what you sense"; no robot at collectionWIYH dataset open-sourced (claims "first embodied VLTA multimodal"); "10× autonomous-driving data volume" (company)UMI/Aria-class human-wearable play, and it did open a dataset (rare for this tier) — but the "10× AV data" and "world-first VLTA" claims are unbenchmarked marketing. (company-reported)
光轮智能 LightWheel数据基建 (合成)Synthetic-data foundry — AIGC + physics sim; supplies sim data to NVIDIA GR00T (N1.5 / GR00T-Dreams), Figure, 1X, ByteDanceRevenue >RMB 100M (2025, company); order book "exponential"China's clearest sell-synthetic-data-as-a-service firm and an NVIDIA ecosystem node — but synthetic data is cheap to replicate (weak moat), and the sim-to-real gap is the buyer's problem, not disclosed. (company-reported) See data-foundry.
国地中心 训练场 (Zhangjiang)数据基建 (state)National-local co-built heterogeneous training ground: 100+ robots from ~10 makers (AgiBot/Fourier/Kepler…) across appliance/auto-assembly + household-manipulation scenarios (fridge-backplate install, food grasping, etc.); ALOHA/mocap/teleop mixPhase-1 ~5,000 m²; target 10M data records in 2026; 2.0 >10,000 m² in H2 2026The state's socialized-cost engine — cross-vendor "heterogeneous" data is its edge over any single lab; but "10M records" is a target, records ≠ curated trajectories, and quality/curation is unproven. (state-reported / target; specific "welding/3C/auto-inspect" scenario list was unverified and removed)
X-Humanoid 北京 (Yizhuang)数据基建 (state)Beijing embodied data/training base: 120+ robot models, 30+ scenarios, mocap-calibrated collection-quality venueSource of RoboMIND / RoboMIND 2.0 (6M+ downloads per Xinhua); raised RMB 700M+ (2026-02)Sets the quality-benchmark end of the state system (mocap-calibrated standards) — the most methodologically serious state site. (state-reported) See data-foundry.
JD.com 京东 (Suqian)数据基建 (大厂)"Data-collection neighborhood" — residents film chores; egocentric human-video at gig scalePlan: 10M hours over 2 years, ~100k employees + ~500k external workersWould dwarf every Western corpus if delivered — but it's a plan; execution and the ~$3/h gig-labor quality are the open questions. (company plan / unverified)

Group profiles

Embodied-AI companies (整机厂 that also collect)

  • 星海图 Galaxea — rig + open dataset. Collects on its own R1 Lite (23-DoF mobile bimanual: 2× 6-DoF A1 arms, 3-DoF torso lift+pitch, 6-DoF omni base, 1.5 m/s) via isomorphic teleoperation (operator kinematics directly retargeted, unlike AgiBot/ByteDance VR-controller retargeting). Galaxea Open-World Dataset: 100k trajectories / 500+ h / 50 scenes / 11 env categories / 150+ task categories / 1,600+ objects / 58 skills. Because it collects on the same commercial hardware researchers can buy, the embodiment gap is smaller than a lab-only rig — but generalization to other morphologies is untested by this dataset alone. Strongest verifiability in the group (arXiv 2509.00576 + HuggingFace OpenGalaxea/Galaxea-Open-World-Dataset; paper reports exactly 100k traj / 500 h / 50 scenes / 150 tasks / 58 skills / 1,600+ objects / 11 sites — all independently confirmed). 400k downloads = press-only; HF card shows ~13k downloads/month (2026-07) (unverified / likely inflated).
  • AgiBot / Galbot / ByteDance — covered in the scoreboard and cross-linked; the three-way split is instructive: AgiBot = centralized humanoid-hall + open data at max scale; Galbot = synthetic-first (SynGrasp-1B), thin on real data; ByteDance = best-documented method (VR human-data + robot teleop, exact rates) but closed. Full device detail for AgiBot's A2 VR/mocap kit is in the device matrix §(D); do not duplicate here.
  • 千寻 Spirit AI / 自变量 X Square / 逐际 LimX / 穹彻 Noematrix — the disclosure-thin tier: named pipelines and device generations, no dataset card/paper/hour count. Spirit's distributed 1,000-device/100-city model is the one genuinely novel architecture (vs centralized halls), but unverifiable.

Hardware vendors (硬件供应商 — the "arms dealers")

  • AgileX 松灵 — Cobot Magic is the single most important entry for the reader's "de-facto Chinese bimanual teleop rig" question. Spec: AgiLex Tracer differential-drive base (685×570×155 mm, 28–30 kg, 100 kg payload — vendor-confirmed) + 4× PiPER lightweight 6-DoF arms (626 mm reach, 1.5 kg payload, 0.1 mm repeatability — AgileX official PiPER spec; the standalone-PiPER 626 mm supersedes the earlier "610 mm" figure, and one Cobot Magic spec-sheet mirror lists 509 mm for the integrated arm) in leader-follower config + 2-finger grippers + Orbbec depth cam (0.3–3 m) + 2 wrist + 1 top camera; runs open-source Mobile-ALOHA software. Price €15,600–€46,200 incl. tax by configuration (来源: generation-robots / robotsinternational listings — verified). This is the commodity that turned the ALOHA recipe into a buyable SKU — and it is why Chinese collection centers scale teleop cheaply. Same tradeoff as ALOHA 2: clean measured labels, but operator-bound (~35 demos/h class). Already noted in the device matrix §(C); here the cost/spec detail is the addition.
  • 帕西尼 PaXini — tactile specialist. DexH13: dual-modal tactile + AI-vision dexterous hand, 1,140 multi-dim tactile units / 3,420 signal channels, 16 DoF (13 active + 3 passive), 15 N fingertip force, 8MP hand-eye camera, 1 mm press accuracy; real-time teleop that converts tactile into visualized digital models. Also GMH18 / DexH5 gloves and the TORA tactile humanoid. Relevance: force/tactile is the one modality video-based family-A rigs cannot recover (see device matrix §5 watch-items); PaXini is a Chinese candidate to supply that subsystem. All figures vendor-reported; "world-first" framing is PR.
  • GenRobot 简智新创 — the resolution of "genrobot" (details below). A pure-play data-collection device vendor whose lineup — DAS Ego (egocentric), DAS Controller (isomorphic teleop, ALOHA-class), DAS Gripper (handheld, UMI-class), DAS Dex, DAS Fingers (tactile) — mirrors the entire A/B/C device taxonomy as buyable products; captures head+hands+full-body across home/factory/logistics, 10,000+ h across 50+ scenarios, plus a "Gen Matrix" data-governance layer. Specs/prices not public.
  • 它石智航 Morphic — SenseHub / TARS — ultra-light human-wearable (TARS-Vision + TARS-Glove), UMI/Aria-class family-A economics (no robot at collection). Notable for actually open-sourcing a dataset (WIYH, claimed "first embodied VLTA multimodal"), unusual for this tier; "10× autonomous-driving data volume" is unbenchmarked.

Data infrastructure (数据基建 — synthetic, facility, service)

  • 光轮智能 LightWheel — the Chinese answer to "sell synthetic data as a service." AIGC + physics-sim pipeline; a named node in NVIDIA's GR00T ecosystem (N1.5 + GR00T-Dreams) with Figure/1X/ByteDance/Alibaba on its client list; revenue >RMB 100M (2025, company). Strategically it is the sim-first counterpart to Galbot's in-house SynGrasp-1B — but externalized as a vendor. Weakness: synthetic data is cheap to replicate → weak moat, and the sim-to-real gap is the buyer's risk. See data-foundry.
  • National/municipal 数据训练场 — the defining Chinese difference. 国地中心 Zhangjiang training ground (national-local co-built, MIIT + Shanghai): first heterogeneous humanoid training ground, "首期已部署超百台异构机器人，来自智元、傅利叶、开普勒等近10家机器人公司" (100+ robots from ~10 makers incl. AgiBot/Fourier/Kepler), documented tasks span appliance/auto assembly (海尔/海信/延锋汽车 partners) and household manipulation (fridge-backplate install, food grasping); ~5,000 m², target 10M data records in 2026 ("今年有望沉淀下1000万条数据"), 2.0 version >10,000 m² ("下半年...开放超1万平方米") in H2 2026. (the earlier "welding/3C/auto-inspect" scenario triad was not supported by the cited sources — removed as unverified.) X-Humanoid Yizhuang (Beijing): 120+ robot models, 30+ scenarios, mocap-calibrated quality standards, source of RoboMIND/RoboMIND 2.0 (6M+ downloads). Plus Shanghai Kylin (~5,000 m²), Beijing Shijingshan (10,000+ m²); 40+ state centers announced, ~two dozen operational (Interact Analysis, early 2026). These socialize collection cost and produce cross-vendor heterogeneous data no single lab can match — the state's structural bet. Caveat: "records" ≠ curated trajectories, targets ≠ delivered, curation/quality unproven. See data-foundry and landscape-china policy timeline (MIIT+SASAC 实景实训 special action, 2026-06-09).
  • JD.com Suqian — 10M-hour gig-egocentric plan; the extreme of cheap-labor scale. Plan, not delivery. (unverified)

genrobot 解析 (what "genrobot" resolved to)

"genrobot" = GenRobot / 简智新创（北京）机器人科技有限公司 (cn.genrobot.com; also branded "简智新创 / GenRobot.AI"). It is a **dedicated data-collection device vendor, product-lined as DAS Ego (first-person/egocentric camera rig), DAS Controller (isomorphic teleoperation controller), DAS Gripper (handheld UMI-class gripper), DAS Dex (dexterous), and DAS Fingers (tactile sensor fingers), plus a Gen Matrix data-governance platform; captures 头+手+全身 (head/hands/full-body) multimodal data across home/factory/logistics, claiming 10,000+ hours over 50+ scenarios. Critical disambiguation: do not confuse it with 智元 AgiBot's "Genie Studio / Genie Operator (GO-1)**" one-stop development platform (zhiyuan-robot.com) — the names collide in search but they are different entities (GenRobot = independent Beijing rig vendor; Genie = AgiBot's software platform + model line). GenRobot is best understood as a Chinese "arms dealer" selling the entire family-A/B/C device taxonomy from the device matrix as off-the-shelf SKUs.

读数 (Synthesis) — how China's data-collection ecosystem differs from the West

  • Structural difference #1 — who pays. The West splits into UMI-class academic rigs (Stanford/Columbia/NYU printing $371 grippers) and private teleop fleets (PI staged Airbnbs, Figure/Tesla operators, Generalist wearables — see data-foundry). China adds a third payer the West lacks: the state. 40+ 数据训练场 socialize collection cost across MIIT/municipal budgets, hardware vendors (who supply robots at a discount to seed centers), and cheap labor (JD gig home-collection $3/h confirmed; training-center operator hourly wages are not disclosed in the cited reporting — the "$5–20/h" range is unverified). The MIIT+SASAC 实景实训 special action (2026-06-09) effectively converts every mandated deployment scenario into a data-collection site — a state purchase order for foundry capacity (landscape-china).
  • Structural difference #2 — hardware-vendor cost-down. The West's family-A rigs are open-source BOMs (anyone prints them); China productizes them. AgileX ships Mobile-ALOHA as a €16k catalog SKU; GenRobot/它石/帕西尼 sell egocentric/teleop/tactile kits as turnkey products. The same 60–70% component supply-chain dominance that cost-reduces humanoids (landscape-china) cost-reduces the rigs. Net: China's comparative advantage is cheap, integrated, buyable collection hardware at volume — the "arms-dealer" wedge the device matrix §4 flags, realized.
  • Structural difference #3 — heterogeneous cross-vendor data. Because state training grounds run 100+ robot models from different makers (~10 vendors at Zhangjiang) on the same scenarios, China uniquely generates cross-embodiment data at facility scale (国地中心 "heterogeneous" ground, X-Humanoid 120+ models). Western fleets are mostly single-embodiment (Figure→Figure, PI→its own robots). Whether heterogeneous data actually trains better cross-embodiment policies is unproven — but it's a bet only the state-coordinated model can place.
  • Open vs closed. China is the open-data counterweight: AgiBot World (1M+ traj), Galaxea Open-World (100k), RoboMIND 2.0, 它石 WIYH are all open releases used as ecosystem subsidies — mirroring Chinese open-weight LLM strategy. Yet the frontier Chinese corpora stay proprietary too (AgiBot's live firehose, Spirit AI's 1,000-device network, Galbot's real-store data). The honest split: China open-sources curated slices to seed the ecosystem; the West's leaders (Generalist 500k h, PI, Figure) open-source nothing. The largest open real-robot corpus is Chinese; the largest corpus period (Generalist, proprietary) is Western — see data-foundry proprietary-vs-open gap.
  • The honest verdict. China's edge is cost structure and coordination, not data quality or verifiability. The three most rigorously documented Chinese efforts (Galaxea, AgiBot World, ByteDance GR-3) all have real papers/dataset cards and are genuinely competitive. But the tier below — Spirit AI's "1,000 devices/100 cities," LimX's "Data Recipe," Noematrix's "CoMiner," JD's "10M hours," and every training-ground "10M records" target — is company/state-reported, single-source, and often a plan or a target rather than a delivered, curated, verifiable dataset. The 2026 embodied-AI funding froth (landscape-china) incentivizes headline scale numbers over disclosed methodology. Read Chinese collection claims the way you read Chinese humanoid shipment claims: the direction (state-scale, cheap, hardware-dense, open-slice) is real and structurally advantaged; the specific magnitudes need primary-source verification. The durable Chinese moat is not any single dataset — it is the vertically integrated stack (own robot + own rig vendor + state-subsidized facility + cheap labor + open-slice flywheel) that no Western player can replicate at the same cost.

来源 (Sources):

  • https://global.agilex.ai/products/cobot-magic — AgileX Cobot Magic: Tracer base + 4× PiPER arms + Orbbec, Mobile-ALOHA teleop rig.
  • https://static.generation-robots.com/media/cobot-magic-spe.pdf — Cobot Magic spec sheet: chassis 685×570×155 mm, 28–30 kg (Tracer base), Orbbec-DABAI depth cam 0.3–3 m. (One mirror of this sheet lists the integrated arm reach as 509 mm — differs from the standalone PiPER 626 mm; do not quote a single arm-reach number without noting the discrepancy.)
  • https://global.agilex.ai/products/piper — AgileX PiPER 6-DoF arm official spec: 626 mm reach, 1.5 kg payload, 0.1 mm repeatability, 4.2 kg (supersedes the earlier "610 mm").
  • https://global.agilex.ai/products/tracer — AgileX Tracer base: 685×570×155 mm, 28–30 kg, 100 kg rated load, differential drive, 1.5 m/s (verified).
  • https://www.robotsinternational.com/Agilex-Cobotmagic-COBOT-Magic.htm — Cobot Magic pricing €15.6k–€46.2k by config; bimanual/whole-body teleop framing.

  • https://huggingface.co/datasets/OpenGalaxea/Galaxea-Open-World-Dataset — Galaxea open dataset card (independent verifiability).
  • https://docs.galaxea-dynamics.com/zh/Guide/R1/hardware_introduction/R1_Hardware_Guide/ — R1/R1 Lite hardware (23-DoF, A1 arms, omni base).

  • https://arxiv.org/pdf/2507.15493 — ByteDance GR-3: PICO 4 Ultra human-VR + Meta Quest teleop, per-task hours, ~450/h vs ~250/h rates.
  • https://cn.genrobot.com/ — GenRobot 简智新创: DAS Ego/Controller/Gripper/Dex/Fingers device lineup, Gen Matrix, 10,000+ h / 50+ scenarios ("genrobot" resolved).
  • https://iqr-robot.com/zh/products/genrobot — GenRobot product distribution page (device taxonomy).
  • http://www.agibot.com.cn/article/188/detail/61.html — AgiBot 智元 Genie Studio one-stop development platform (data-collect + train + sim + inference modules); the entity NOT to conflate with GenRobot. (zhiyuan-robot.com now 301-redirects here — verified.)
  • https://mall.paxini.com/product/dex/66f27ea530cd11a8e9d1d5ff — PaXini DexH13 product listing: 1,140 tactile units / 3,420 channels / 16 DoF (13 active + 3 passive) / 15N fingertip / 8MP hand-eye / 1mm press accuracy (numbers verified here).
  • https://paxini.com/dex/gen2 — PaXini DexH13/DexH5 tactile dexterous hands (product family page).
  • https://www.leaderobot.com/news/4819 — PaXini DexH13 tactile+vision hand writeup; NOTE this writeup lists variant figures (978 sensors / 3,912 channels / 13 DoF) that conflict with the current product listing above — cite the mall listing for the 1,140/3,420/16-DoF numbers.
  • https://www.geekpark.net/news/358631 — 它石智航 SenseHub / WIYH open dataset ("first embodied VLTA multimodal", company claim).
  • https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/1985762777881659086 — 它石 TARS SenseHub (TARS-Vision + TARS-Glove) wearable kit description.
  • https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/1976772838896719216 — 光轮智能 LightWheel: >RMB 100M revenue, synthetic-data business, A/A+ rounds.
  • https://blogs.nvidia.cn/blog/gt00t-dreams-lightwheel/ — NVIDIA GR00T-Dreams × LightWheel synthetic-data partnership.
  • https://www.shanghai.gov.cn/nw4411/20250501/1127ab41648c404c87013e8db06e3a14.html — 国地中心 Zhangjiang heterogeneous training ground: 100+ robots, target 10M records 2026.
  • https://www.shanghai.gov.cn/nw4411/20250211/5e981bfbee7840c6b3056d9d3a6cceea.html — Zhangjiang training ground phase-1: ~5,000 m², "超百台异构机器人，来自智元、傅利叶、开普勒等近10家" makers; documented tasks = fridge-backplate install / food grasping / soccer + appliance-auto assembly (海尔/海信/延锋). (Source does NOT state welding/3C/auto-inspect.)
  • https://finance.sina.cn/stock/jdts/2026-06-16/detail-inicrivm7531991.d.html — Zhangjiang 2.0 >10,000 m² H2 2026 supply-chain-conference update.
  • https://english.news.cn/20260613/5d8906d63b2a4586a04fe160bd5d7a65/c.html — X-Humanoid Yizhuang base: 120+ robot models, RoboMIND, mocap-calibrated standards.
  • https://restofworld.org/2026/china-ai-robotics-training-data/ — JD Suqian 10M-hour plan; Chinese collection gig-labor economics ($3/h).
  • https://restofworld.org/2026/china-robots-training-centers-workers/ — 40+ state centers announced by Dec, ~two dozen operational (Interact Analysis). NOTE: this article does NOT give a training-center hourly wage; the only source-confirmed rate is JD gig home-collection 20 yuan/$3-h (other article). The "$5–20/h" figure is unverified.

4. 读数 (Synthesis) — the core tension

  • The fundamental tradeoff is not going away. Embodiment-free rigs (UMI-class) are cheap ($25–$400), fast (~111 demos/h, ~3× teleop), and crowd-scalable — but they carry an embodiment gap (a handheld gripper/hand/walking-human is not the deployment robot's arm/hand/base) and recovered, not measured, action labels (SLAM/VIO/ARKit estimates the pose → 6mm–24mm error, drift, and failure modes enter the training data). Teleop rigs (ALOHA/DROID/AgiBot) give exact measured joint actions on (or morphologically near) the deployment robot — but cost ~$22k/station (stationary ALOHA 2; ~$32k only with a mobile base) plus a human, and hit a hard ~35 demos/h operator-fatigue ceiling (iDP3's authors state plainly that AVP teleop is "tiring for human teleoperators, making imitation data hard to scale up"). You cannot have cheap-and-scalable and clean-and-zero-gap from one device.
  • Where each is winning in 2026-07: family A (robot-free/human) is winning the pretraining scale war — Generalist 500k+ h, Figure Go-Big, NVIDIA EgoScale's log-linear dexterity law all say cheap human data at volume moves the needle. Family B/C (teleop/on-robot) is winning the last mile — reliability, contact-rich, force-critical tasks still need clean labels, and RL-from-teleop-corrections (π*0.6/RECAP) is the current best reliability lever. The field has converged on pretrain-cheap, fine-tune-clean hybrids; no serious lab bets on one family.
  • The trend line: the device count in family A is exploding (UMI → FastUMI → DexUMI → ActiveUMI → Mobile-UMI → HoMMI → Dobb·E → DexCap → Generalist/Genesis gloves), each variant chipping at a specific limit — mirrors (depth), T265 (calibration), exo+inpainting (dexterous gap), chest-cam (mobile base), gloves (tactile+dexterity). But every variant that adds capability (dexterous hands, mobile bases) widens the embodiment gap it must then patch, which is why the hard problems (force, exact kinematics, human-gait→wheeled-base) recur. Teleop is not being replaced; it is being relegated to the last mile where labels must be exact.
  • The ODM / hardware-supplier read (Quectel-style "arms dealer" wedge): there is a real hardware-supplier opportunity, but it is a low-margin, volume niche, not a moat.
    • The bull case: every lab and every one of China's 40+ state data centers needs physical rigs — grippers, wrist-cam mounts, RealSense/T265 modules, capture cards, mocap gloves, leader-follower arms, tactile e-skin. UMI/Fast-UMI/Dobb·E are literally BOMs of commodity parts (3D-printed gripper, GoPro, RealSense, iPhone, ARX/Piper arms, Rokoko gloves). A module maker that ships a calibrated, ROS-ready, quality-controlled capture kit (the thing labs actually struggle with — per-device IMU-cam calibration, drift QA, LeRobot-format output) sells into a market that is scaling with the entire data-foundry buildout.
    • The bear case: the winning rigs are deliberately cheap and open-source — UMI is $371 of parts anyone can print; the whole point is commodity access. Margins live in integration and QA (calibration, drift detection, format compliance), not in the parts. The value is migrating from hardware to the data itself (the corpus is the moat, not the rig — see Data foundry), so a pure rig supplier is selling picks-and-shovels in a gold rush where the picks cost $25. The durable play is tactile/sensor modules (the one thing family-A rigs mostly lack and can't fake — force is not in video), precision tracking modules (the fidelity bottleneck), and calibrated turnkey kits for the volume centers — a components-and-integration business, not a platform business.
    • The tell: the sensor modules that matter most (RealSense T265/L515, GoPro IMU, ZED stereo, Rokoko EMF, tactile e-skin) are exactly where an established module-maker already has manufacturing scale — the wedge is supplying the sensing subsystem of the rig, not the rig.

5. Watch items

  • Tactile-in-the-rig: force/tactile is the one signal video-based family-A rigs structurally cannot capture (DexUMI, DexCap-EMF, Genesis glove are early answers). Whoever makes cheap, robust, rig-integrable tactile skin unlocks contact-rich data at scale. See Manipulation tactile section.
  • Tracking-fidelity floor: UMI's SLAM (6mm) vs FastUMI's T265 (24mm) vs ARKit (black-box) — the position-error floor of cheap tracking bounds how good robot-free labels can be. Watch VIO/SLAM module improvements.
  • Mobile-base gap: Mobile-UMI / HoMMI / UMI-on-Legs all attack human-locomotion → robot-base transfer differently; none is verified/replicated. Which approach (sim WBC vs direct SE(2) recovery) wins is open.
  • Dexterous-hand gap closure: does the Genesis 1:1:1 glove or DexUMI's exo+inpainting actually close the human-hand → robot-hand gap, or is it patched-not-solved? All current evidence is single-source/company-reported.
  • Rig commoditization vs QA moat: does a "calibrated capture kit" business emerge, or do labs keep printing their own $371 rigs and pushing value entirely to the corpus?

  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10329 — UMI: $73 gripper + $298 GoPro rig (~$371), ORB-SLAM3 fork, 6.1mm/3.5° ATE, ~111 demos/h, side mirrors, zero-shot UR5e/Franka. (sq: primary)

  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.19499 — Fast-UMI: T265 direct pose (task-dependent ~10.5–24.1mm error, not a single 2.37cm), hardware-decoupled ~5 grippers, no SLAM, Elgato capture card, 20Hz. (sq: primary)

  • https://umi-on-legs.github.io/ — UMI-on-Legs project page. (sq: primary)
  • https://arxiv.org/html/2605.20894v1 — Mobile UMI: chest+wrist dual-cam, ChArUco anchor, SE(3)+SE(2) decomposition, AgiLex Tracer 2.0 + Piper, 83.8% avg. (sq: single-source)

  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.16098 — Dobb·E "The Stick": $25 reacher-grabber + iPhone + ARKit, HoNY 13h/22 homes/1.5M frames, 81% success. (sq: primary)
  • https://dobb-e.com/ — Dobb·E project page: 5-min task learning, open framework. (sq: primary)
  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.07788 — DexCap: Rokoko EMF gloves + per-glove SLAM cam + chest L515 + 3× T265, backpack NUC, 60Hz, ~40 min/session, occlusion-robust. (sq: single-source)
  • https://dex-cap.github.io/ — DexCap project page and BOM context. (sq: single-source)
  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.02292 — ALOHA 2: 2× ViperX + 2× WidowX (Trossen), 4× D405 cams, 14-DoF @50Hz, low-friction rail. Paper states NO cost. (sq: primary; hardware)
  • https://www.roboticscenter.ai/blog/aloha-robot-guide — third-party ALOHA BOM: 2× ViperX 300 ≈ $11k + 2× WidowX 250 ≈ $6.4k = ~$17.4k arms; stationary complete ~$21.8–22.1k; ~$32k only with AgiLex Tracer mobile base (Mobile-ALOHA). (sq: secondary, cost)
  • https://global.agilex.ai/products/cobot-magic — AgileX Cobot Magic: 4-arm Mobile-ALOHA leader-follower, mobile base, top+wrist cams. (sq: vendor)
  • https://arxiv.org/html/2511.17441v1 — RoboCOIN: confirms Cobot Magic / leader-follower / exo / mocap as collection methods in a bimanual dataset. (sq: verified)
  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.12945 — DROID: Franka Panda + Quest 2 6-DoF teleop, ZED 2/Mini cams, 15Hz, 76k trajectories/350h/564 scenes/13 labs. (sq: primary)
  • https://droid-dataset.github.io/ — DROID project/hardware page. (sq: primary)
  • https://arxiv.org/html/2503.06669v1 — AgiBot World Colosseo: G1 VR + whole-body mocap teleop, 6-axis F/T, 8 cameras, RGBD+LiDAR, 100 robots, ~4,000 m². (sq: primary)
  • https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10803 — iDP3: AVP teleop of real Fourier GR1 humanoid + head L515 LiDAR, 10 demos/task; verbatim "tiring… making imitation data hard to scale up" (NOT robot-free). (sq: primary)
  • https://humanoid-manipulation.github.io/ — iDP3 project page. (sq: primary)

(sq: company-reported, unverified)

  • https://www.figure.ai/news/project-go-big — Figure Go-Big: egocentric human video, Brookfield 100k+ units, 100%-human-video Helix navigation claim. (sq: company-reported)
